qa_pairs.question: What is the basic command to execute a program inside an Apptainer container?
qa_pairs.answer: To run a program inside an Apptainer container, you use the `apptainer run` command, followed by the name of your SIF file (e.g., `your-container-name.sif`), and then the command you wish to execute within the container.

qa_pairs.question: Can you provide an example of how to compile and then run a C++ program within an Apptainer container?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, to compile a C++ program like `myprog.cpp` inside a container named `your-container-name.sif` using `g++`, you would use the command: `apptainer run your-container-name.sif g++ -O2 -march=broadwell ./myprog.cpp`. Subsequently, to run the compiled program (e.g., `a.out`) from the same container, you would use: `apptainer run your-container-name.sif ./a.out`.
